errors.fellOffEdge: Oh no - you tried to fall off the edge of the maze!
errors.hitWall: Ouch - you walked into a wall!

checks.notReachedEnd: You didn't reach the end of the maze.
checks.turnAroundNotDefined: You need to define a turnAround function at the top of your code.
checks.turnAroundNotCalled: You've defined turnAround, but you need to actually call it in your final else block.
checks.maxLines: Your program should be no more than 17 lines of code.

turnAround.name: Turn around at dead ends
turnAround.description: Define a turnAround() function at the top of your code that calls turnLeft() twice, then use it in the final else block. Your solver should escape the dead ends in all three mazes.

bonusShortSolution.name: Bonus: keep it short
bonusShortSolution.description: Solve a maze with two dead ends, keeping your whole program to 17 lines of code or fewer.

maze1.name: A single dead end
maze1.description: A straight corridor with one dead-end spur that forces a turn-around

maze2.name: A dead end and a turn
maze2.description: A winding path with a dead-end spur before turning to the exit

maze3.name: Keep it short
maze3.description: Try and ensure your whole final program is 17 lines of code.

defineFunction.question: How do I define a new function?
defineFunction.answer: Use the `function` keyword followed by the name, parentheses for inputs, and a body in curly braces, e.g. `function turnAround() { ... }`.

placement.question: Where should I put the function definition?
placement.answer: Put it at the top of your code, before the loop. Functions must be defined before they're called.

turnAroundBody.question: What should go inside `turnAround()`?
turnAroundBody.answer: Just two calls to `turnLeft()`. Two left turns add up to facing the opposite direction.

useInSolver.question: How do I use my new function in the maze solver?
useInSolver.answer: Find the `else` block in your previous solution where you called `turnLeft()` twice and replace those two calls with a single `turnAround()` call.

move.description: Moves the character **one step forward** in the current direction. Walking into a wall ends your program with an error, so check what's ahead before moving!

turnLeft.description: Turns the character **90 degrees to the left** (counterclockwise). This changes the direction the character is facing.

turnRight.description: Turns the character **90 degrees to the right** (clockwise). This changes the direction the character is facing.

canMove.description: Returns **true** if the space ahead of the character is not a wall, **false** otherwise.

canTurnLeft.description: Returns **true** if the space to the character's left is not a wall, **false** otherwise.

canTurnRight.description: Returns **true** if the space to the character's right is not a wall, **false** otherwise.
